steps.title: Identify what the audience needs
steps.content: The audience already knows the Atlantic Monthly, so an effective introduction should skip explaining the magazine and instead focus on what's NEW: what Halverson's book explores.

steps.title: Skip redundant background and focus on the book's content
steps.content: Choice A states the book's subject directly—female authors whose autobiographies appeared in the early 1900s—without re-explaining the magazine itself. Choices B and C both re-introduce the magazine's founding date and editorial scope (contradicting the audience's stated familiarity), while choice D provides only a single author example rather than the book's overall focus. That matches choice A — A is correct.
